entry.title: Washington Post: 14 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine takes center stage in Asia’s geopolitical debates
  • Russia makes progress in the east as Ukraine pleads for weapons
  • Ukrainian troops struggle to hold back Russian advance in east
  • Global nuclear arsenal expected to grow for first time since Cold War
  • Boris Johnson wants to junk parts of Brexit deal on Northern Ireland
  • Family says bodies found in search for journalist and colleague
  • Trudeau tests positive for coronavirus days after meeting Biden
  • Philippines dispatch: Pigs, presstitutes and how journalists are harassed
  • Costly, scandal-ridden mass testing is China’s latest ‘zero covid’ bet
  • Afghans go hungry as U.S. and Taliban officials blame each other
  • North Korea fires artillery shells after arms pledge, South Korea says
  • Russia likely to seize control of eastern Ukrainian region within weeks, U.S. official says
  • Iran media: Scores injured in explosion at chemical factory
  • Court refuses to stop UK from deporting migrants to Rwanda
  • Mexican soldiers find 50 meth labs in first half of June
  • Search continues for missing men in Brazilian Amazon
  • Congo military accuses Rwanda of invasion; rebels seize town
  • US sanctions 93 Nicaraguan officials for crackdown
  • 55 people killed in latest attack in northern Burkina Faso
  • Pakistan journalist apparently taken by plainclothes police
  • Jihadi attacks mount in Burkina Faso despite junta's efforts
  • European star survey reveals celestial treasure trove
  • Proud Boys disrupt drag-queen reading event, prompting hate-crime probe
  • Assistant police chief gets $1.5 million payout after hanging Nazi symbol
  • A Minneapolis woman was about to be evicted. Neighbors bought her home for her.
  • Newspaper apologizes to Rebel Wilson over plans to out her relationship
  • Trump’s inner circle warned him election-fraud claims were false
  • Bill Stepien broke with Trump after 2020 — but not all his candidates did
  • As hearing’s star witness, Barr says Trump was ‘detached from reality’
  • Man accused in Kavanaugh plot texted sister before surrender, police say
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • Jan. 6 hearing recap: Trump pushed ‘big lie’ despite being told election fraud claims were false, aides testify
  • Inside Biden’s frustration with soaring prices
  • Yes, there’s a tampon shortage. Here’s why.
  • S&P 500 slumps into bear market, Dow dives again as 2022 losses accelerate
  • Crypto bank’s freeze on withdrawals sends major currencies down
  • Pump shock: Why gas prices are so high
  • Amazon calls cops, fires workers in attempts to stop unionization nationwide
  • FAA will allow SpaceX to launch Starship from Texas, but with a catch
  • Microsoft enters agreement to respect Activision Blizzard unionization
  • You agreed to what? Doctor check-in software harvests your health data.
  • Looking to rent your home? Here’s what to consider.
  • IRS rules about capital gains may lead to questions. We have answers.
  • Historic, four-bedroom house in Charles County lists at $355,000
  • Ambani’s Consumer Empire Gets a Cricket Spin
  • The ECB Needs a Bazooka to Close Bond Spreads
  • The Fed Has No Choice But to Let This Tantrum Rip
  • New HSBC Breakup Plan Is Still Deeply Unconvincing
  • Why Closer Ties Between Russia and China Have Democracies Worried
  • How the ‘Energy Shock’ Has Upended the Global Economy
  • The Boom in Private Markets Has Transformed Finance. Here’s How
  • Why Measuring the ‘Scope’ of Carbon Emissions Is Tricky
  • Trump and Fox News told the ‘big lie’ for profit
  • Ron DeSantis’s drag show comments foretell a vile trend
  • Trump disbanded ‘Team Normal’ — and brought in woozy Rudy
  • How the gay rights movement found such stunning success
  • The Jan. 6 committee proved that Biden won, but is that really the point?
  • We can’t let Ukraine lose. It needs a lot more aid, starting with artillery.
  • Truth is emerging in a journalist’s death. Palestinians could reveal more.
  • Trump’s aides told him the truth. Now they are finally telling us.
  • The Federal Reserve should go big now to fight inflation
  • The Jan. 6 committee exposed Trump’s lies — and indicted the GOP in the process
  • The gun deal would represent real reform. If the GOP doesn’t kill it.
  • Why are my patients still isolating in their homes? Gun violence.
  • My guide dog helps keep me safe. But she can’t protect me from guns.
  • Drunk with power
  • Cartoon by Scott Stantis
  • How will gun violence affect the Virginia midterms?
  • Pronouns are not determined by biology
  • In trying to go green, the U.S. sometimes can’t get out of its own way
  • Years of outrage have yet to move the needle on guns
  • A new bridge will be great, but we can still use the old bridge
  • College coaches are focusing on the wrong issue
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 14 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 14 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-14 05:31AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • For women, Matt Kean might just be the teal deal
  • Boss says employees can go home if worker putts golf ball into cup
  • Wiggins unleashes to give Warriors the edge
  • Australia news LIVE: ASX enters correction with $116b wiped off sharemarket; Blackouts possible in NSW and Queensland; Socceroos off to the World Cup
  • Regional house price growth outstrips Melbourne, Sydney
  • Time and place for upmarket apartment buyers is now, says developer
  • From ‘gross girl’ to ‘clean girl’: The merry-go-round of TikTok’s ‘girl’ tropes
  • ‘Money was tight growing up, but I saved and invested, so I have choices’
  • Aliens attack the human race on Falling Skies
  • Justin Bieber reveals half his face is paralysed
  • From the Archives, 1992: Brett Whiteley’s sudden death stuns art world
  • ‘The greatest thing he has ever been a part of’: Vindication for Arnold, Socceroos as they slay the doubters

  • Why the top-earning Australian at Norman’s first event says LIV golf is here to stay

  • Man charged with murder after fatal stabbing in Sydney’s west
  • Boys falling far behind girls in HSC and at university
  • ‘Technical issues’ hobble age verification for same-day alcohol delivery
  • To be fair, lock investors out of land tax calculation

  • Blue suede shoes on the catwalk

  • Seven Summits, Seven Flights

  • Family waits for ID of bodies found in Amazon search for missing pair

  • Trump became 'detached from reality' hearing reveals
  • ‘For his own good’: Prince William, Charles urged Queen to disinvite Prince Andrew
  • Days after Uvalde massacre, Ohio approves armed school employees
  • Russia pushes Ukrainian forces back from the centre of Severodonetsk
  • WHO considers renaming monkeypox virus to minimise racism, stigma
  • Fifty years on, Australia and China can renew their diplomatic vows
  • Marles vows to continue flying over Paracels, increase military exercises with Japan
  • From the Archives, 1982: War over, talks begin again
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • US lawmakers strike deal on gun reform
  • Ukrainian teen used hobby drone to help target Russian tanks with artillery
  • Violent threats against Jacinda Ardern surge
  • Death of Netflix reality show participant inspires Japanese laws to jail online bullies
  • WA’s new top cop announced
  • Australian stock market falls dramatically
  • Defence Minister Richard Marles holds talks in Japan
  • Woman dies in caravan fire in Melbourne’s south east
  • Major funding boost for NSW childcare sector
  • Energy crisis gripping eastern Australia at risk of worsening
  • Man charged with murder after woman was stabbed to death in south-west Sydney
  • Convicted paedophile and 'Hey Dad' actor Robert Hughes walks free from prison
  • Star’s lawyers say it wasn’t suitable for licence, but it is now
  • Former cop accused of leaking secrets from police reports fronts court
  • Gun found after father’s ‘execution’ in front of teen daughter
  • Queensland government to review Star’s casino licence suitability
  • Police confiscate Western Australia's first 3D-printed gun
  • Bloodbath: ASX enters correction with $116b wiped off sharemarket
  • On high alert: Markets a sea of red as they wait for the big reveal
  • Inside the secretive world of shipping Russia’s tainted oil
  • Has pop culture pariah WeWork turned the corner? This Aussie investor thinks so
  • ‘Vacuum’ on crypto regulation opens door to influencers
  • Fertility firm boss happy to ‘have the chat’ with potential suitors
  • Aussie home values are about to tumble. We should let them
  • Lachlan Murdoch’s $220m bet on digital bookmaking falls flat
  • ‘Party like a Russian’ turns toxic at Putin’s economic forum
  • Flight fight: Rex blames cancelled routes on ‘predatory’ Qantas
  • Volatile energy market creates blackout risks in NSW, Queensland
  • ‘It’s really hard’: Farmers feel squeeze of rising power bills
  • Why Neneh Cherry had no interest in a duet with Nicki Minaj
  • Pride and Prejudice meets its match in joyous reboot of Austen’s classic
  • How Bluey went from a kids TV show to a global merchandising machine
  • The seven best World Cup anthems
  • Perfume Genius review: Charismatic showman comes up smelling of roses

  • What is a derecho?
  • National weather forecast for Tuesday June 14

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 14 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Prosecution again acquits singer over sexual assault allegations

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Malaysia partially lifts chicken export ban, Singapore importers bring in kampung and black chickens from Jun 14

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 14 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 14 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-14 05:22AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • August 14, 1998: ‘Billions spent to prop up shares’ - Hong Kong fights back amid financial crisis
  • Hong Kong’s iconic Jumbo Floating Restaurant leaves for new secret location as residents bid farewell
  • Hong Kong ‘will not give in a single inch’ to requests by business chambers, consulates to ease Covid rules, Lam says, citing state officials’ visit and transition of power
  • July 1, 1997: ‘The Handover’ - Hong Kong’s return to China after British rule
  • HKEX hoped alchemy would turn acquisition of century-old London Metal Exchange into ‘super-connector’ gold. What went wrong?
  • Global stocks slump to bear market for the first time since March 2020 as key MSCI index falls 21 per cent from its November record
  • July 22, 2009: Bank payouts on Lehman Brothers minibonds to top HK$6 billion
  • Hongkongers are living longer than ever, but experts flag health problems ahead
  • ‘If everybody criticises you, you probably got it right’: ex-chief justice Geoffrey Ma reflects on challenges for Hong Kong’s courts
  • Hong Kong’s uncomfortable silence on Queen Elizabeth’s platinum jubilee
  • Hong Kong was not British colony as China did not recognise unequal treaties ceding city to Britain, new textbooks reveal
  • Scammers con 16 Hongkongers out of HK$4.29 million in recent months, use stolen personal information to apply for fraudulent loans
  • October 12, 2016: ‘Declaration of war as Hong Kong Legco opens’
  • Over a third of Hong Kong tech workers expect a pay rise of at least 10pc but city’s employers only willing to give 3pc: survey
  • June 18, 2015: Hong Kong government’s reform plan fails, as does Legco walkout
  • Hong Kong’s privacy watchdog takes down 90 per cent of social media posts deemed to constitute doxxing under new law
  • June 12, 2013: US spy net targets Hong Kong, Edward Snowden reveals to SCMP
  • Consumption vouchers: Hong Kong scheme extended to give non-PR residents, foreign students and ‘talents’ HK$5,000 handout
  • September 28, 2014: Hong Kong police fire tear gas as thousands join Occupy Central
  • Hong Kong police search for girls after video showing 12-year-old slapped 20 times in the face goes viral
  • Police offer HK$250,000 reward for information over shootout in busy nightlife district in Hong Kong
  • October 27, 2012: Non-locals in Hong Kong hit with new property tax
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong schools report highest number of infections since return to classrooms
  • September 12, 2005: Dazzling start to Hong Kong Disneyland carnival
  • Chinese police on anti-gang blitz after attack on women in Tangshan
  • Pixar’s Lightyear won’t show in some countries over same-sex kiss, with China screening also unlikely
  • US lawmakers reach compromise on screening investments in China
  • Two top US and China officials meet for a third time this year to ‘reduce risks’
  • China economy: with more US tightening on the horizon, could this be Beijing’s last chance to cut rates in 2022?
  • Ancient treasure trove sheds light on mysterious Chinese kingdom
  • No one, China included, wants to see the Ukraine war going nuclear
  • China develops AI that ‘can use deception to hunt satellites’
  • Chinese exporters stay resilient amid increased competition from Vietnam, other emerging markets, Alibaba executive says
  • China GDP: nearly 11 billion Covid tests seen giving economy a US$26 billion boost in second quarter
  • China’s struggling private firms tasked with lifting nation of out economic doldrums
  • China has major vaccine role to play in future health challenges: Gavi
  • China insists it has sovereign rights over Taiwan Strait
  • China and Pakistan, jolted by Karachi attack that killed Chinese, vow stronger military and security ties
  • ‘Resolutely resist violence’: UK fashion label Boy London denounces brutality after publicly linked with gang who viciously attacked women in north China
  • China’s largest academic paper database CNKI opens some services to individuals after Beijing’s antitrust probe
  • China and Australia break diplomatic drought with ‘frank’ defence ministers talks
  • Beijing’s ban on Taiwanese grouper imports prompts emergency measures to help island’s fish farmers
  • Chinese capital tightens Covid controls to stamp out spread of bar cluster
  • Huawei’s cloud service down for half an hour, disrupting services for Guangdong customers
  • Australia-China relations: Albanese says Beijing must lift sanctions on exports to reset ties
  • US defence chief denies seeking to split Asia ‘into hostile blocks’ during Thailand trip
  • Ukraine war fallout: Pakistan plunged into darkness as Europe gobbles up world’s LNG supplies
  • Marcos’ ‘confessions’: Philippines president-elect admits to ‘trolls’, needing guidance – and doing it for his parents
  • UN human rights chief Bachelet says no to second term, ‘no relationship’ to China trip
  • North Korea ‘has 20 nuclear warheads at most’, security think tank says
  • Japan’s health conscious turn to ‘swamp’ food in bid to lose weight but feel full
  • Is Japan’s reopening to tourists triggering a series of ‘kamikaze car’ attacks between yakuza gangs?
  • Japan passes bill to make online insults punishable by jail time, following suicide of Hana Kimura, star of Netflix show ‘Terrace House’
  • Singapore’s Lawrence Wong serves as acting PM as Lee Hsien Loong takes a week-long break
  • UK sending first asylum seekers to Rwanda after London court refuses to halt deportation plan
  • EU to tackle deepfakes, with Big Tech like Google, Facebook, Twitter facing fines of 6 per cent of turnover
  • Congo military accuses Rwanda of invasion as rebels capture town
  • Kevin Spacey to appear in London court over sex offences charges
  • Google offers to let rival ad firms place ads on YouTube to address EU antitrust probe
  • Bear market hits Wall Street as stocks, bonds and cryptocurrencies dive
  • Donald Trump ‘detached from reality’ over election, top aides tell second Capitol riot hearing
  • WHO considers renaming monkeypox over fears of stigma and racism
  • US Capitol riot hearing to focus on Trump’s ‘Big Lie’ election fraud
  • No vaccine, no can do: Rudy Giuliani’s son Andrew must join NY political debate virtually, because he’s not been jabbed
  • UK defies EU by scrapping Northern Ireland post-Brexit trade rules
  • Russia’s ex-PM says Vladimir Putin ‘out of it’, Ukraine war could last two years
  • Google suspends engineer who publicly claimed he interacted with a sentient AI bot
  • No way out of Ukraine’s Sievierodonetsk after Russia destroys all bridges
  • Google pays US$118 million to settle gender discrimination lawsuit that says it underpaid women
  • As China’s economy revs up, brace for even higher global energy prices and inflation
  • How rezoning of Hong Kong’s industrial sites for residential use could provide the city with homes it desperately needs
  • Hong Kong stocks slip as global markets dive into bear market on Fed tightening overdrive, China lockdown concerns
  • Hong Kong fund managers’ lobby group urges incoming Chief Executive John Lee to drop quarantine rules
  • Has the Covid-19 pandemic finally pushed Japan towards flexible work arrangements?
  • Hong Kong stocks on track to trounce mainland peers as outlook for China’s tech giants brightens
  • Major Chinese cities see more land auctions being scrapped, as broke developers hobbled by ‘three red lines’ stay away
  • Chinese cryptocurrency-mining rig maker Nano Labs eyes US$50 million Nasdaq IPO defying clampdown at home
  • Goldman sees end of selling in Chinese stocks, but CCB, Everbright say only volatility is assured
  • US bill to rein in Big Tech backed by dozens of small and big companies
  • Man who knows where to find the best pizza in Hong Kong on his rating system, pies that surprised, and plans to meet his followers
  • When jury members in Hong Kong received death threat over manslaughter conviction for deadly knife and arson attack
  • How luxury handbag label Delvaux, the ‘Hermès of Belgium’, made an incredible comeback, with China the key
  • Lightyear movie review: Pixar’s Toy Story spin-off featuring Chris Evans’ voice is aimed squarely at children
  • 5 fashion collections that celebrate Pride Month and the LGBT community, from Tom Daley sweater NFT to Cher T-shirt with Versace
  • From high cholesterol, high blood pressure and prediabetes to healthiest he’s ever been – how an ex-CEO turned his health around
  • Chinese Nezha legend inspires Hong Kong Dance Company production that’s inventive and inspiring
  • Luxury dog kennel design competition draws Foster + Partners architects, former Apple design chief and bespoke furniture maker David Linley among others
  • A dream come true for Gin Lee: pop, jazz and tango battle it out in a Le French May concert in Hong Kong unlike anything she has done before
  • 5 K-drama stars with odd jobs before the fame: Kim Tae-ri worked in a supermarket while Kim Min-seok was a cook in a Japanese restaurant … what about So Ji-sub, Go Min-si and Pyo Ye-jin?
  • Why are waiting lists for luxury watches getting even longer? Collectors can queue up to 10 years for the most exclusive Rolex, Patek Phillipe or Audemars Piguet
  • Inside Tamannaah Bhatia’s luxury life: the millionaire Tollywood star lives in a US$2 million Mumbai complex, drives a BMW, and owns one of the world’s biggest diamonds
  • Meet Brooke Shields’ 19-year-old daughter, Rowan Henchy: the budding journalist wore her mum’s glam 1998 Golden Globes dress to prom – but will she follow in her Hollywood footsteps?
  • Where has Star Wars’ Hayden Christensen been? He’s back as Anakin Skywalker in Disney+’s Obi-Wan Kenobi, but after early fame – and dating Natalie Portman – he traded Hollywood for life on the farm
  • The 8 best steak and grill restaurants in Hong Kong, from the Grand Hyatt Steakhouse and Ocean Park Marriott Hotel’s Prohibition Grill House & Cocktail Bar, to Rosewood Hong Kong’s Henry
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 14 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Search for clues as bodies of hundreds of little blue penguins wash ashore in New Zealand
  • The cartoon where it happens: Lin-Manuel Miranda guest stars as horse in new Bluey episode
  • Global markets plunge as Fed mulls biggest rate rise in decades
  • Improved mental health for children who play well with peers by age three
  • ‘A merry-go-round of buck-passing’: inside the four-year Grenfell inquiry
  • The Grenfell inquiry revealed shocking failures – five years on, they are far from fixed | Lucie Heath
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Donbas battles ‘most brutal’ Europe has seen, Zelenskiy says; civilians trapped in Sievierodonetsk – live
  • South Korea truckers’ strike threatens to disrupt Samsung chip production
  • Andrew Redmayne: from a barista course and the brink of retirement to World Cup qualification | Emma Kemp
  • Warriors move to within one win of NBA title after Game 5 victory over Celtics
  • We needed China deal to protect ‘domestic security’, says key Solomon Islands official
  • Australian goalkeeper Andrew Redmayne dances Socceroos into World Cup finals
  • Record flooding and mudslides force closure of Yellowstone national park
  • Lizzo removes ‘harmful’ ableist slur from new song GRRRLS after criticism
  • Pound dog blues: puppy has surgery after swallowing 20 coins from owner’s purse
  • How to Murder Your Husband writer gets life in prison for husband’s murder
  • Wildfires erupt in Arizona and California in foreboding sign of intense summer
  • ‘Incel’ who killed 11 in Toronto van murders sentenced to life in prison
  • Harry Kane’s hunger for competition drives him on despite lack of rest
  • Nations League roundup: Luka Modric penalty gives Croatia win in France
  • Mickelson says he has ‘deep empathy’ for 9/11 victims after LIV Series criticism
  • Joe Root’s day four cameo shows he is a master craftsman at his pinnacle | Andy Bull
  • A picture in time: the Beatles arrive in Adelaide on their 1964 Australian tour
  • At least 55 killed by militants in latest attack in Burkina Faso
  • Philip Baker Hall: a character actor of strength, gravitas and lugubrious good humour
  • Clearly concussed footballers are being allowed to play on, Ifab claims
  • Andy Murray hopeful for Wimbledon after withdrawal from Queen’s
  • The writer and the activist: how Dom Phillips and Bruno Pereira bonded over the Amazon
  • Bolsonaro says ‘something wicked’ done to Dom Phillips and Bruno Pereira
  • Top aides repeatedly told Trump fraud claims were baseless, Jan 6 panel hears
  • Actor Philip Baker Hall, known for Magnolia and Seinfeld, dies at 90
  • How do you get dressed in the morning? Four very different approaches to putting on clothes
  • Fears grow for Sievierodonetsk civilians as plant struck and bridges destroyed
  • More than 15,000 millionaires expected to leave Russia in 2022
  • EU poised to take legal action against UK over Northern Ireland protocol bill
  • UN appeals to public for $20m to stop feared catastrophic oil spill from tanker
  • Amy Schumer among stars urging change in Hollywood gun portrayals
  • Ukraine asks the west for huge rise in heavy artillery supply
  • Former tennis player Jelena Dokic says she ‘almost jumped off balcony’ in April
  • A journalist trying to out Rebel Wilson was like something from the sordid 80s. Thankfully, the world has changed | Zoe Williams
  • Did you solve it? When Wordle curdles
  • Julee Cruise obituary
  • ‘I hadn’t slept for 10 days’ – the Lemonheads on making It’s a Shame About Ray
  • Amber Heard calls out ‘unfair’ role of social media in Johnny Depp case
  • How does Google’s AI chatbot work – and could it be sentient?
  • Real age versus biological age: the startups revealing how old we really are
  • Violent BJP rhetoric and bulldozing of homes show Indian Muslim dilemma
  • The WTO, with its ‘market knows best’ ideology, has failed. It’s time to bury it | Nick Dearden
  • The US baby formula shortage isn’t bad luck. It was completely preventable | Matt Stoller
  • Two shows dominated the Tony awards and proved Broadway’s debt to British taxpayers
  • The big idea: why we shouldn’t be levelling up
  • Patriotism, optimism and striding ahead: the often overlooked story of Muslim Britain | Miqdaad Versi
  • How we met: ‘I noticed she was transgender, and remember thinking she seemed sweet and shy’
  • Can’t face another sad salad? Here’s how to ditch the lettuce and forage for something more exciting
  • Weather tracker: heatwaves could add to energy problems across Europe
  • ‘How can I not get enraged?’ Russian director Serebrennikov on war, exile and his new opera
  • ‘Ferocious’ Covid outbreak in Beijing traced to raucous bar
  • Don’t be complacent, another Covid wave is coming. Here’s how we can manage it | Devi Sridhar
  • Best running gear: top gadgets to keep you motivated
  • A new start after 60: ‘Selling vintage clothes takes me back to happier days’
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
